patch.value: Architecture boundary gate: src/cli and src/mcp may import only from src/sdk — lint rule + CI enforcement

Once primitives are promoted, enforce the layering from ADR pm-muhw mechanically: an import-boundary lint rule (no src/core deep imports from src/cli|src/mcp) wired into the static quality gate so the SDK-first architecture cannot regress. Duplicate check: no existing boundary-gate item; static-quality-gate exists but has no import-boundary rule.

Lint/gate rule fails CI on any src/core import from src/cli or src/mcp; allowlist starts at current state and ratchets down per promotion task, reaching zero; rule documented in AGENTS/docs architecture section

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Pre-push Greptile review 92d93b38-8ef1-46a9-9b8f-4e9b2f36eefc follow-up: addressed both findings before push. The scanner now recursively detects string-literal dynamic import() calls into src/core instead of only top-level import/export declarations, and baseline-read failures now return empty violation arrays with error populated so JSON consumers do not confuse scan failure with new import violations. Focused static-quality-gate spec and full coverage reran successfully after the fix.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: PR #431 follow-up: GitHub Windows regression (Node 24) failed because the SDK-boundary unit test forced node:path to POSIX while using a Windows drive-letter temp root, causing collectPrivateCoreImportEdges to resolve no candidates. Fixed the test to exercise the native path implementation for the SDK import-boundary scenario. Verification after fix: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/scripts/release/static-quality-gate.spec.ts (62 passed); node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (263 files, 5411 tests, 100/100/100/100); pnpm lint; pnpm security:scan; git diff --check; pnpm changelog:pm:check.

patch.value: Extended scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs with an SDK import-boundary ratchet backed by scripts/release/sdk-import-boundary-baseline.json and regression coverage. The gate fails on new private src/core imports from src/cli or src/mcp, unsupported computed dynamic imports, and stale baseline entries.

Static quality gate fails CI on new src/core import edges from src/cli/src/mcp, computed dynamic imports from boundary sources, or stale baseline entries; allowlist starts at current state and ratchets down per promotion task, reaching zero when all primitives are promoted; rule documented in architecture docs and SDK inventory

Enforce the SDK-first migration mechanically with a static quality ratchet: src/cli and src/mcp may not add new private src/core import edges, may not use computed dynamic imports that bypass the inventory, and must remove stale baseline entries as primitives move into src/sdk. Duplicate check: no existing boundary-gate item; static-quality-gate existed but had no import-boundary ratchet.
